title: The 2×2 Matrix of Feedback Topologies
description: A 2×2 grid with Sampling type (Voltage/Shunt vs Current/Series) on one axis and Mixing type (Series/Voltage vs Shunt/Current) on the other. Each cell shows the topology name, amplifier type, and impedance effects.
details: Grid layout: rows = Mixing (Series, Shunt), columns = Sampling (Shunt, Series). Cell (Series,Shunt) = 'Voltage Amp, R_i↑ R_o↓'. Cell (Series,Series) = 'Transconductance, R_i↑ R_o↑'. Cell (Shunt,Shunt) = 'Transresistance, R_i↓ R_o↓'. Cell (Shunt,Series) = 'Current Amp, R_i↓ R_o↑'. Color code up/down arrows.

title: Voltage Sampling (Shunt) — Like a Voltmeter
description: Diagram showing the feedback network connected in parallel across the output. The amplifier block (A) has output voltage V_o, and the feedback block (β) has its input connected across V_o. Show the feedback network 'seeing' the output voltage.
details: Amplifier A block with V_o output. β block connected in parallel across V_o. Show R_L also connected across V_o. Arrows showing V_o enters β, and V_f = βV_o exits. Analogy: voltmeter symbol in parallel.

title: Current Sampling (Series) — Like an Ammeter
description: Diagram showing the feedback element in series with the output current path. The output current I_o flows through the feedback element, creating V_f across it.
details: Amplifier A block with I_o output. β block (a resistor) in series with the output current path. I_o flows through β. Show V_f = βI_o across the element. Analogy: ammeter symbol in series.

title: Series Mixing — Voltages Add by KVL
description: Circuit showing V_S, V_f, and V_i in a series loop at the input. KVL gives V_i = V_S - V_f. The feedback voltage subtracts from the source voltage.
details: Input loop: V_S source → V_f feedback voltage (opposing) → V_i appears across amplifier input R_i. Show KVL loop equation. Highlight that voltages are in series.

title: Shunt Mixing — Currents Add by KCL
description: Circuit showing I_S, I_f, and I_i meeting at the input node. KCL gives I_i = I_S - I_f. The feedback current subtracts from the source current.
details: Node diagram: I_S flowing in, I_f flowing out (or in opposing direction), I_i flowing into R_i. KCL equation at node. Highlight that currents meet at a node.

title: Series-Shunt Feedback (Voltage Amplifier)
description: Complete feedback amplifier model for the voltage amplifier: Thévenin source, series mixing of V_S and V_f, amplifier with A_V gain and R_i/R_o, shunt sampling at output with R_L, and β feedback network connected across V_o.
details: Full circuit: V_S with R_S → series loop with V_f → into R_i → A_V·V_i source with R_o → V_o across R_L. β network from V_o producing V_f back to series loop. Label all impedances and signals.

title: Series-Series Feedback (Transconductance Amplifier)
description: Feedback model for transconductance amplifier: Thévenin source, series mixing, amplifier with G_M gain, series sampling where I_o flows through feedback element, and high output impedance.
details: V_S → series loop → R_i → G_M·V_i current source with R_o (large) → I_o through R_L and through β element (in series). V_f developed across β element feeds back to series loop.

title: Shunt-Shunt Feedback (Transresistance Amplifier)
description: Feedback model for transresistance amplifier: Norton source, shunt mixing of I_S and I_f at input node, amplifier with R_M gain, and shunt sampling at output.
details: I_S current source with R_S → node where I_f subtracts → I_i into R_i (small) → R_M·I_i voltage source with R_o (small) → V_o across R_L. β network: I_f = V_o/R_f back to input node.

title: Shunt-Series Feedback (Current Amplifier)
description: Feedback model for current amplifier: Norton source, shunt mixing at input, amplifier with A_I gain, and series sampling where output current flows through feedback element.
details: I_S → input node (KCL with I_f) → R_i (small) → A_I·I_i current source with R_o (large) → I_o through R_L and β element. I_f from β feeds back to input node.

title: Complete Summary: All Four Feedback Topologies
description: A comprehensive table or card layout showing all four topologies side by side with: topology name, amplifier type, mixing/sampling, impedance effects, gain formula, and β units.
details: Four columns, one per topology. Rows: Name, Amplifier Type, Input Signal, Output Signal, Mixing, Sampling, R_if formula, R_of formula, β units, Gain formula. Use color coding: green for impedance increases, red for decreases.

title: Flowchart: Identifying the Feedback Topology
description: A decision-tree flowchart starting with 'Find the feedback element', then branching on 'Is it connected directly to the output?' (Yes=Voltage/Shunt, No=Current/Series), then 'Is it connected directly to the input?' (Yes=Current/Shunt, No=Voltage/Series), leading to one of the four topologies.
details: Start → 'Identify R_f' → 'Connected to output node directly?' → Yes: 'Voltage Sampling (Shunt)' / No: 'Current Sampling (Series)' → 'Connected to input node directly?' → Yes: 'Shunt Mixing (Current)' / No: 'Series Mixing (Voltage)' → Combine answers → Topology name and amplifier type.

title: Emitter Follower as Series-Shunt Feedback — Interactive
description: A Falstad simulation of a common-collector (emitter follower) circuit, with annotations showing the feedback path through R_E. Students can see the gain is ≈1, input impedance is high, and output impedance is low.
details: Single BJT in CC configuration with R_E. Show V_in and V_out scopes. Annotate R_E as the feedback element. Display gain value (≈1), R_in (high), R_out (low). Optional: toggle R_E bypass cap to show effect of removing feedback at AC.

title: Shunt-Shunt Feedback with R_f from Collector to Base
description: A Falstad simulation of a CE amplifier with R_f from collector to base. Students can see the transresistance behavior — current in, voltage out — and observe the reduced input and output impedances.
details: Single CE BJT with R_f from V_C to base. Input is a current source. Show V_out scope. Display R_in (low), R_out (low), and gain. Toggle R_f to see feedback on/off effect.
